transcript.text: I think I spend way too much time on my phone

llmCalls.prompt: {"text":"I think I spend way too much time on my phone"}

Plan the BACKGROUND JOB action for job kind: daily_brief.
You are routing a background tick for an executive assistant.
Decide whether the assistant should take an action right now, and if so
which action and through which channel. The assistant MUST NOT execute
sensitive actions directly — anything that contacts a person, modifies a
calendar, books travel, makes a call, runs a workflow, or spends money
returns requiresApproval=true so the user can confirm.

Return ONLY valid JSON with exactly these fields:
{"action":"send_message"|"send_email"|"schedule_event"|"modify_event"|"cancel_event"|"book_travel"|"make_call"|"execute_workflow"|"spend_money"|"noop",
"channel":"telegram"|"discord"|"slack"|"imessage"|"sms"|"email"|"google_calendar"|"browser"|"phone"|"internal",
"requiresApproval":true|false,
"reason":"short justification",
"payload":{...action-specific fields, or empty object when action=noop}}

Rules:
- Choose action=noop when no action is warranted this tick (e.g. nothing overdue).
- requiresApproval=true for any action that touches a person or external system.
- requiresApproval=false ONLY when action=noop or the action is purely internal (logging, internal workflow with no side effects).
- The reason must explain WHY this tick warrants the chosen action, in any language.

Job trigger: proactive_tick
Subject user: c91d1a60-eca4-0479-864b-2aa8d4e73197
Available channels: ["internal"]
Snapshot: {"now":"2026-04-18T06:00:06.732Z","timezone":"America/Los_Angeles"}
llmCalls.response:
```json
{
  "action": "noop",
  "channel": "internal",
  "requiresApproval": false,
  "reason": "No overdue tasks or events that require action at this time.",
  "payload": {}
}
```

Explanation:

- The job trigger is `proactive_tick`, which implies that the system is checking for tasks or events that require attention regardless of their due dates.
- The subject user is `c91d1a60-eca4-0479-864b-2aa8d4e73197`, but there is no information about their tasks or events.
- The available channels are only `internal`, which is suitable for logging or internal workflows without side effects.
- Since no task or event is overdue and there is no information about the subject user's tasks or events, no action is warranted at this time.

# Available Actions

- GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT: T8d — Per-app time breakdown for the last N hours (default 24h). Returns noDataReason='macos-only' on non-Darwin platforms.

- GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS: Admin-only. Check whether a local hosts-file website block is currently active and when it ends.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_GET_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_SELFCONTROL, IS_BLOCK_RUNNING
  example: User: "Is there a website block running right now?" -> actions: GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.
  aliases[14]: CHECK_INBOX, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_DIGEST, INBOX_RESPOND, TRIAGE_INBOX, SCAN_MESSAGES, CHECK_MESSAGES, DAILY_DIGEST, DAILY_BRIEF, INBOX_SUMMARY, REPLY_INBOX, RESPOND_TO_MESSAGE, MISSED_CALL_FOLLOWUP, GROUP_CHAT_HANDOFF
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.

  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known — eliminates name ambiguity.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; note?:string - Optional note about the interaction.

- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).

- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.

  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.

- DOSSIER: Generate a pre-meeting or person-background briefing dossier with context about attendees, recent interactions, and upcoming event details. Use this for requests like 'pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella', 'give me the background on the person I'm meeting next: Julia Chen', or 'brief me for my next meeting'. If the user explicitly wants a brief, backgrounder, prep sheet, or dossier, use this action instead of replying from ENTITIES, FACTS, or memory alone.

  params[5]: intent?:string - Natural language request. - UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.

- PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES: Propose candidate meeting time slots to offer to another person. Use this for bundled scheduling while traveling or when you need concrete reschedule options.
  aliases[5]: SUGGEST_MEETING_TIMES, OFFER_MEETING_SLOTS, FIND_MEETING_SLOTS, PROPOSE_SLOTS, BUNDLE_MEETINGS_WHILE_TRAVELING
  params[5]: durationMinutes?:number - Meeting length in minutes. Defaults to the owner's configured default duration.; daysAhead?:number - Number of days ahead to search. Defaults to 7. Ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied.; slotCount?:number - Number of candidate slots to return. Defaults to 3.; windowStart?:string - Optional ISO-8601 earliest start for the search window.; windowEnd?:string - Optional ISO-8601 latest end for the search window.

- GENERATE_DOSSIER: Low-level event-specific dossier generation for an already identified calendar event. Use this only when the event id or fuzzy event title is already known from a prior workflow. Prefer DOSSIER for generic user requests like 'brief me', 'background on the person I'm meeting', or 'prep me for my next meeting'.

  params[3]: eventId?:string - Calendar event id. Mutually optional with eventTitleFuzzy.; eventTitleFuzzy?:string - Fuzzy title match used when eventId is unknown (e.g. 'my 3pm').; windowDays?:number - Context window in days (default 7).

- BLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Start a local website block by editing the system hosts file. Use this for fixed-duration or generic focus blocks like 'block twitter and reddit for the next 2 hours', 'turn on a focus block for all social media sites', or 'block youtube'. Use recent conversation context to block public websites like x.com for a fixed duration or until manually unblocked. Do not use this when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, or todo; that is BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE. If the user confirms a block in a follow-up message without repeating the hostnames, reuse that context through the action planner.
  aliases[11]: SELFCONTROL_BLOCK_WEBSITES, BLOCK_WEBSITE, BLOCK_SITE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_NOW, WEBSITE_BLOCKER, WEBSITEBLOCKER, START_FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_SITES, FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_SOCIAL_MEDIA, SOCIAL_MEDIA_BLOCK
  params[2]: websites?:array of string - Website hostnames or URLs to block, for example ['x.com', 'twitter.com']. When omitted, the planner can recover them from recent conversation context.; durationMinutes?:number [default=60] - How long to block those websites, in minutes. Omit this to use the default duration.

- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.

  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.

  params[6]: timeZone?:string - IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.; preferredStartLocal?:string - Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; preferredEndLocal?:string - Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; defaultDurationMinutes?:number - Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).; travelBufferMinutes?:number - Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).; blackoutWindows?:array - Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
  example: User: "No calls between 11pm and 8am unless I explicitly say it's okay." -> actions: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).

- X_READ: Read X/Twitter DMs, the home timeline or mentions feed, or run a recent search. Use this for requests like 'check my Twitter DMs', 'what's on my X timeline?', 'show me my mentions', or 'search Twitter for posts about elizaOS'. Use this for retrieving content from X, not posting. Do not reply that X/Twitter access is unavailable when this action is registered and visible.
  aliases[10]: READ_X, READ_TWITTER, CHECK_TWITTER_DMS, TWITTER_TIMELINE, X_TIMELINE, TWITTER_MENTIONS, SEARCH_TWITTER, X_DMS, X_FEED, X_SEARCH
  params[5]: subaction?:string [values=read_dms|read_feed|search] - X read operation. read_dms for direct messages, read_feed for timeline/mentions, search for recent tweet search.; intent?:string - Free-text description of the request, e.g. "check my X DMs", "show my mentions", "search X for elizaOS".; query?:string - Search query string for the search subaction.; feedType?:string [values=home_timeline|mentions|search] - Feed to read for read_feed. One of home_timeline (default), mentions, or search.; limit?:number - Max items to return (1-100).
  example: User: "Check my X DMs." -> actions: X_READ
- SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS: Search across every connected channel — Gmail, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, X DMs, Calendly — plus agent memory. Returns merged hits with citations to source platform, room, and timestamp. Connectors without native search emit typed unsupported markers (no fabricated results). Admin/owner only.
  aliases[5]: CROSS_CHANNEL_SEARCH, SEARCH_ALL_CHANNELS, SEARCH_EVERYWHERE, FIND_ACROSS_PLATFORMS, UNIFIED_SEARCH
  params[8]: query?:string - Exact search phrase. When omitted, the LLM extracts a query from `intent`.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of what to search for. Multilingual.; person?:string - Display name of the person to focus the search on. Resolves via the relationships graph when available.; primaryEntityId?:string - Pre-resolved canonical cluster primary entity id (UUID).; startIso?:string - Lower-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; endIso?:string - Upper-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; channels?:array of string - Channel allowlist. Allowed values: gmail, memory, telegram, discord, imessage, whatsapp, signal, x-dm, calendly, calendar.; limit?:number - Per-channel hit cap (default 10).
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[4]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: Add a domain to the autofill whitelist. Requires explicit user confirmation (confirmed: true). Persisted to the local profile store.
  aliases[2]: TRUST_SITE_FOR_AUTOFILL, APPROVE_AUTOFILL_DOMAIN
  params[2]: domain?:string - Domain to add. Stored as a registrable domain (e.g. 'example.com'). Subdomains are covered by the parent entry.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true. Required to ensure the user approved the addition, not the agent.
- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Use search_events for flights, itinerary, travel, appointments, or keyword lookup; feed for agenda/schedule reads; next_event for the next upcoming event; create_event only when creating a new event.; intent?:string - Natural language calendar request, especially schedule or itinerary questions. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION
- NONE: Respond but perform no additional action. This is the default if the agent is speaking and not doing anything additional.
  aliases[5]: NO_ACTION, NO_RESPONSE, NO_REACTION, NOOP, PASS
  example: User: "Hey whats up" -> actions: NONE
- LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS: List currently active remote-control sessions.
  aliases[3]: REMOTE_SESSIONS, ACTIVE_REMOTE_SESSIONS, REMOTE_LIST
  example: User: "Are any remote sessions open?" -> actions: LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS
- LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS: List all currently active website block rules, including their gate type and gate target. Only use this for website/app blocking status. Do not use it for inbox blockers, message priority, daily briefs, or general executive-assistant triage.
  aliases[3]: LIST_BLOCK_RULES, SHOW_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, WEBSITE_BLOCKS_STATUS
  example: User: "What website blocks are active right now?" -> actions: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling coordinator. Manages negotiations with another party across proposal/response rounds: start a negotiation, propose times, record responses, finalize the confirmed slot, or cancel.
  aliases[4]: SCHEDULE_MEETING, NEGOTIATE_MEETING, COORDINATE_SCHEDULE, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Possible response actions: UNBLOCK_APPS, SUBSCRIPTIONS, DOSSIER, CALL_EXTERNAL, SCHEDULING, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, PASSWORD_MANAGER, PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES, LIFE, CHECK_AVAILABILITY, LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, REVOKE_REMOTE_SESSION, SCREEN_TIME, REQUEST_FIELD_FILL, BLOCK_APPS, IGNORE, TWILIO_VOICE_CALL, GET_TIME_ON_SITE, RELATIONSHIP, GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT, LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS, GET_APP_BLOCK_STATUS, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER, LIST_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, CALL_USER, UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, BLOCK_WEBSITES, MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER, RUN_MORNING_CHECKIN, CALENDLY, REMOTE_DESKTOP, RELEASE_BLOCK, UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE, CALENDAR_ACTION, REPLY, INBOX, SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD, NONE, START_REMOTE_SESSION, MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE, CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND, GENERATE_DOSSIER, REGISTER_BROWSER_SESSION, GET_TIME_ON_APP, SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS, LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN, INTENT_SYNC, HEALTH, BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE, FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, X_READ, UPDATE_ENTITY, GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS

# Available Actions
actions[57]:
- GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT: T8d — Per-app time breakdown for the last N hours (default 24h). Returns noDataReason='macos-only' on non-Darwin platforms.
  aliases[3]: ACTIVITY_REPORT, WHAT_DID_I_WORK_ON, TIME_TRACKING_REPORT
  params[1]: windowHours?:number - Number of hours of history to report on (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "What did I work on today?" -> actions: GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT
- SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD: Set the follow-up threshold (in days) for a specific contact. Requires a positive integer threshold and either contactId or an unambiguous contactName.
  aliases[3]: FOLLOWUP_RULE, CHANGE_FOLLOWUP_INTERVAL, SET_CONTACT_FREQUENCY_DAYS
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; thresholdDays:number - Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.
  example: User: "Remind me to follow up with Dana every 14 days" -> actions: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
- GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS: Admin-only. Check whether a local hosts-file website block is currently active and when it ends.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_GET_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_SELFCONTROL, IS_BLOCK_RUNNING
  example: User: "Is there a website block running right now?" -> actions: GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.
  aliases[14]: CHECK_INBOX, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_DIGEST, INBOX_RESPOND, TRIAGE_INBOX, SCAN_MESSAGES, CHECK_MESSAGES, DAILY_DIGEST, DAILY_BRIEF, INBOX_SUMMARY, REPLY_INBOX, RESPOND_TO_MESSAGE, MISSED_CALL_FOLLOWUP, GROUP_CHAT_HANDOFF
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.
  example: User: "Triage my inbox" -> actions: INBOX
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE: Mark a contact as followed-up-with (updates lastContactedAt to now). Requires either an explicit contactId (UUID) or an unambiguous contactName. Ambiguous names return a clarifying response without modifying any contact.
  aliases[5]: FOLLOWED_UP, FOLLOWUP_DONE, CONTACTED, MARK_CONTACTED, RECORD_INTERACTION
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known — eliminates name ambiguity.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; note?:string - Optional note about the interaction.
  example: User: "I just talked to Alice, mark it done" -> actions: MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. This includes durable travel-booking preferences or a reusable travel-preference checklist when the owner wants those preferences remembered for future bookings. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.
  aliases[7]: SAVE_OWNER_PROFILE, SET_OWNER_PROFILE, UPDATE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_USER_PROFILE, SAVE_TRAVEL_PREFERENCES, TRAVEL_PROFILE, BOOKING_PREFERENCES
  params[8]: name?:string - The owner's preferred name.; relationshipStatus?:string - Relationship status such as single, partnered, married, or n/a.; partnerName?:string - Partner's name when known, otherwise omit or use n/a.; orientation?:string - Owner orientation when clearly stated.; gender?:string - Owner gender when clearly stated.; age?:string - Owner age or stable age descriptor when clearly stated.; location?:string - Owner location when clearly stated.; travelBookingPreferences?:string - Reusable flight and hotel preference checklist or summary to remember for future bookings.
  example: User: "Set up a list of my flight and hotel preferences so you don't have to ask every time." -> actions: UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE
- DOSSIER: Generate a pre-meeting or person-background briefing dossier with context about attendees, recent interactions, and upcoming event details. Use this for requests like 'pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella', 'give me the background on the person I'm meeting next: Julia Chen', or 'brief me for my next meeting'. If the user explicitly wants a brief, backgrounder, prep sheet, or dossier, use this action instead of replying from ENTITIES, FACTS, or memory alone.
  aliases[9]: GENERATE_DOSSIER, PREMEETING_BRIEFING, MEETING_BRIEFING, PREPARE_FOR_MEETING, BRIEF_ME, BACKGROUND_BRIEF, PERSON_BACKGROUND, NEXT_MEETING_BRIEF, WHO_AM_I_MEETING
  params[5]: intent?:string - Natural language request. Examples: "brief me for my 2pm with Alice", "prep me for tomorrow's board meeting".; calendarEventId?:string - Optional calendar event id to pull event details (title, time, location, attendees).; subject?:string - Subject line for the dossier (e.g. meeting title or topic).; attendeeHandles?:array of string - List of attendee handles/emails to look up in the relationships table.; generatedForAt?:string - ISO timestamp the dossier is generated for (defaults to now).
  example: User: "Pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella" -> actions: DOSSIER
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES: Propose candidate meeting time slots to offer to another person. Reads the owner's calendar busy times and meeting preferences (preferred hours, blackout windows, travel buffer) and returns three available slots by default over the next seven days. Use this for bundled scheduling while traveling or when you need concrete reschedule options.
  aliases[5]: SUGGEST_MEETING_TIMES, OFFER_MEETING_SLOTS, FIND_MEETING_SLOTS, PROPOSE_SLOTS, BUNDLE_MEETINGS_WHILE_TRAVELING
  params[5]: durationMinutes?:number - Meeting length in minutes. Defaults to the owner's configured default duration.; daysAhead?:number - Number of days ahead to search. Defaults to 7. Ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied.; slotCount?:number - Number of candidate slots to return. Defaults to 3.; windowStart?:string - Optional ISO-8601 earliest start for the search window.; windowEnd?:string - Optional ISO-8601 latest end for the search window.
  example: User: "While I'm traveling, try to bundle meetings with PendingReality and Ryan on the same day if possible." -> actions: PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES
- GENERATE_DOSSIER: Low-level event-specific dossier generation for an already identified calendar event. Use this only when the event id or fuzzy event title is already known from a prior workflow. Prefer DOSSIER for generic user requests like 'brief me', 'background on the person I'm meeting', or 'prep me for my next meeting'.
  aliases[3]: MEETING_DOSSIER_BY_EVENT, EVENT_DOSSIER, GENERATE_DOSSIER_FROM_EVENT
  params[3]: eventId?:string - Calendar event id. Mutually optional with eventTitleFuzzy.; eventTitleFuzzy?:string - Fuzzy title match used when eventId is unknown (e.g. 'my 3pm').; windowDays?:number - Context window in days (default 7).
  example: User: "Generate a dossier for event 'Board sync tomorrow'" -> actions: GENERATE_DOSSIER
- BLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Start a local website block by editing the system hosts file. Use this for fixed-duration or generic focus blocks like 'block twitter and reddit for the next 2 hours', 'turn on a focus block for all social media sites', or 'block youtube'. Use recent conversation context to block public websites like x.com for a fixed duration or until manually unblocked. Do not use this when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, or todo; that is BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE. If the user confirms a block in a follow-up message without repeating the hostnames, reuse that context through the action planner.
  aliases[11]: SELFCONTROL_BLOCK_WEBSITES, BLOCK_WEBSITE, BLOCK_SITE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_NOW, WEBSITE_BLOCKER, WEBSITEBLOCKER, START_FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_SITES, FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_SOCIAL_MEDIA, SOCIAL_MEDIA_BLOCK
  params[2]: websites?:array of string - Website hostnames or URLs to block, for example ['x.com', 'twitter.com']. When omitted, the planner can recover them from recent conversation context.; durationMinutes?:number [default=60] - How long to block those websites, in minutes. Omit this to use the default duration.
  example: User: "Block x.com and twitter.com for 2 hours." -> actions: BLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.
  aliases[7]: SET_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SAVE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SET_PREFERRED_TIMES, SET_BLACKOUT_WINDOWS, SLEEP_WINDOW, NO_CALL_HOURS, PROTECT_SLEEP
  params[6]: timeZone?:string - IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.; preferredStartLocal?:string - Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; preferredEndLocal?:string - Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; defaultDurationMinutes?:number - Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).; travelBufferMinutes?:number - Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).; blackoutWindows?:array - Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
  example: User: "No calls between 11pm and 8am unless I explicitly say it's okay." -> actions: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
- PASSWORD_MANAGER: Look up or copy credentials from your password manager (1Password CLI or ProtonPass). Use this for requests like 'look up my GitHub password' or 'show me my saved logins for github.com'. Subactions: search, list, inject_username, inject_password. Credentials are NEVER displayed in chat — injection only copies to the OS clipboard briefly.
  aliases[6]: ONEPASSWORD, CREDENTIAL_LOOKUP, COPY_CREDENTIAL, LOOK_UP_PASSWORD, SHOW_SAVED_LOGINS, LIST_LOGINS
  params[7]: subaction?:string - One of: search, list, inject_username, inject_password.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of the lookup intent.; query?:string - Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.; itemId?:string - Password manager item id (required for inject_* subactions).; field?:string - Which field to inject when using a generic inject subaction. Ignored when subaction explicitly names the field.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true to copy a credential to the clipboard.; limit?:number - Optional item limit for the `list` subaction (default 20).
  example: User: "Find my GitHub login" -> actions: PASSWORD_MANAGER
- X_READ: Read X/Twitter DMs, the home timeline or mentions feed, or run a recent search. Use this for requests like 'check my Twitter DMs', 'what's on my X timeline?', 'show me my mentions', or 'search Twitter for posts about elizaOS'. Use this for retrieving content from X, not posting. Do not reply that X/Twitter access is unavailable when this action is registered and visible.
  aliases[10]: READ_X, READ_TWITTER, CHECK_TWITTER_DMS, TWITTER_TIMELINE, X_TIMELINE, TWITTER_MENTIONS, SEARCH_TWITTER, X_DMS, X_FEED, X_SEARCH
  params[5]: subaction?:string [values=read_dms|read_feed|search] - X read operation. read_dms for direct messages, read_feed for timeline/mentions, search for recent tweet search.; intent?:string - Free-text description of the request, e.g. "check my X DMs", "show my mentions", "search X for elizaOS".; query?:string - Search query string for the search subaction.; feedType?:string [values=home_timeline|mentions|search] - Feed to read for read_feed. One of home_timeline (default), mentions, or search.; limit?:number - Max items to return (1-100).
  example: User: "Check my X DMs." -> actions: X_READ
- SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS: Search across every connected channel — Gmail, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, X DMs, Calendly — plus agent memory. Returns merged hits with citations to source platform, room, and timestamp. Connectors without native search emit typed unsupported markers (no fabricated results). Admin/owner only.
  aliases[5]: CROSS_CHANNEL_SEARCH, SEARCH_ALL_CHANNELS, SEARCH_EVERYWHERE, FIND_ACROSS_PLATFORMS, UNIFIED_SEARCH
  params[8]: query?:string - Exact search phrase. When omitted, the LLM extracts a query from `intent`.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of what to search for. Multilingual.; person?:string - Display name of the person to focus the search on. Resolves via the relationships graph when available.; primaryEntityId?:string - Pre-resolved canonical cluster primary entity id (UUID).; startIso?:string - Lower-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; endIso?:string - Upper-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; channels?:array of string - Channel allowlist. Allowed values: gmail, memory, telegram, discord, imessage, whatsapp, signal, x-dm, calendly, calendar.; limit?:number - Per-channel hit cap (default 10).
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[4]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: Add a domain to the autofill whitelist. Requires explicit user confirmation (confirmed: true). Persisted to the local profile store.
  aliases[2]: TRUST_SITE_FOR_AUTOFILL, APPROVE_AUTOFILL_DOMAIN
  params[2]: domain?:string - Domain to add. Stored as a registrable domain (e.g. 'example.com'). Subdomains are covered by the parent entry.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true. Required to ensure the user approved the addition, not the agent.
- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Use search_events for flights, itinerary, travel, appointments, or keyword lookup; feed for agenda/schedule reads; next_event for the next upcoming event; create_event only when creating a new event.; intent?:string - Natural language calendar request, especially schedule or itinerary questions. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION
- NONE: Respond but perform no additional action. This is the default if the agent is speaking and not doing anything additional.
  aliases[5]: NO_ACTION, NO_RESPONSE, NO_REACTION, NOOP, PASS
  example: User: "Hey whats up" -> actions: NONE
- LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS: List currently active remote-control sessions.
  aliases[3]: REMOTE_SESSIONS, ACTIVE_REMOTE_SESSIONS, REMOTE_LIST
  example: User: "Are any remote sessions open?" -> actions: LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS
- LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS: List all currently active website block rules, including their gate type and gate target. Only use this for website/app blocking status. Do not use it for inbox blockers, message priority, daily briefs, or general executive-assistant triage.
  aliases[3]: LIST_BLOCK_RULES, SHOW_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, WEBSITE_BLOCKS_STATUS
  example: User: "What website blocks are active right now?" -> actions: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling coordinator. Manages negotiations with another party across proposal/response rounds: start a negotiation, propose times, record responses, finalize the confirmed slot, or cancel.
  aliases[4]: SCHEDULE_MEETING, NEGOTIATE_MEETING, COORDINATE_SCHEDULE, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE

  aliases[3]: BROADCAST_INTENT, SYNC_INTENT, CROSS_DEVICE_INTENT
  params[12]: subaction:string [values=broadcast|list_pending|acknowledge|prune_expired] - Which operation to perform. One of: broadcast, list_pending, acknowledge, prune_expired.; intent?:string - User-phrased intent summary (optional, for logging).; kind?:string [values=user_action_requested|routine_reminder|attention_request|state_sync] - Intent kind.   - Prefer facts about the current user/sender that will still matter in a week: identity, stable preferences, recurring collaborators, durable setup, long-term projects, or ongoing constraints.
  - Do NOT extract temporary status updates, current debugging/work items, one-off session metrics, isolated praise/complaints, or facts that are only true right now.
  - If a fact would feel stale, irrelevant, or surprising to store a week from now, skip it.
  - When in doubt, omit the fact.
3. Identify and describe relationships between entities.
  - The sourceEntityId is the UUID of the entity initiating the interaction.
  - The targetEntityId is the UUID of the entity being interacted with.
  - Relationships are one-direction, so a friendship would be two entity relationships where each entity is both the source and the target of the other.
4. It is normal to return no facts when nothing durable was learned.
5. Always decide whether the user's task or request is actually complete right now.
  - Set `task_completed: true` only if the user no longer needs additional action or follow-up from you in this turn.
  - If you asked a clarifying question, an action failed, work is still pending, or you only partially completed the request, set `task_completed: false`.
6. Always include a short `task_completion_reason` grounded in the conversation and action results.

Output:
TOON only. Return exactly one TOON document. No prose before or after it. No <think>.
Do not output JSON, XML, Markdown fences, or commentary.
Use indexed TOON fields exactly like this:
thought: "a self-reflective thought on the conversation"
task_completed: false
task_completion_reason: "The request is still incomplete because the needed action has not happened yet."
facts[0]:
  claim: durable factual statement
  type: fact
  in_bio: false
  already_known: false
relationships[0]:
  sourceEntityId: entity_initiating_interaction
  targetEntityId: entity_being_interacted_with
  tags[0]: dm_interaction

For additional entries, increment the index: facts[1], relationships[1], tags[1], etc.
Always include `task_completed` and `task_completion_reason`.
If there are no durable new facts, omit all facts[...] entries.
If there are no relationships, omit all relationships[...] entries.

IMPORTANT: Your response must ONLY contain the TOON document above. Do not include any text, thinking, or reasoning before or after it.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD
